$AMZN Zoox unveiled the “next evolution” of its robotaxi ahead of a wider U.S. rollout.

Updates include upgraded touchscreens, more comfortable seats, improved headrests and interior tweaks designed to make the ride more passenger-friendly.

I’m confident heading into $MU earnings today and $AAPL reported 20% price increase tied to memory is a major reason why.

Apple is the most sophisticated, highest-leverage memory buyer in consumer tech so when a buyer with that much scale and pricing power says cost increases are “unavoidable” then it signals to me this is likely a multi-year elevated pricing cycle. 

You don’t reset flagship pricing and take the customer-perception hit unless your supply chain team believes elevated memory costs are likely to persist across the product cycle.
$SPCX will join the Nasdaq 100 on July 6.

SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son says he expects $ARM value to rise 10x as AI workloads become more CPU-intensive.

He also called $INTC manufacturing business critical as tech giants seek alternatives to $TSM with potential $NVDA and $AAPL deals helping drive Intel’s value higher.
Nancy Pelosi disclosed new buys of up to $5M in $INTC and up to $1M in $UBER.

$AVGO and OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño which is OpenAI’s first custom AI chip built for LLM inference.

Broadcom says this is the start of a multi-generation roadmap for gigawatt-scale AI data centers with $MSFT and other partners beginning in 2026.

$QCOM has acquired Modular for ~$3.9B in stock to add a hardware-agnostic AI software and compiler layer to its platform.

Modular helps AI run efficiently across different chip architectures strengthening Qualcomm’s push beyond connectivity into a fuller AI compute stack.

Agility Robotics is going public via $CCXI at a $2.5B valuation and will trade under $AGLT.

The Digit humanoid robot maker is backed by $NVDA, $AMZN, SoftBank and Foxconn with Nvidia selecting Agility as the launch partner for its Halos physical AI safety system.

Japan announced a $2.3T AI and semiconductor roadmap through fiscal 2040 focused on chips, physical AI & industry-specific vertical AI.

The U.S. is spending trillions on AI infrastructure, China is spending trillions and now Japan is committing massive capital to the same race.

$TSLA and $RUN announced a 16 GW distributed energy partnership built around home batteries, smart thermostats and connected devices.

The companies say the capacity requires no new hardware, water or land from offtakers with 300 MW+ already available in Virginia.

$NBIS launched Nebius AI Cloud Aether 3.6 with an AI agent for cloud infrastructure control plus stronger enterprise governance and major storage improvements.

Nebius is pushing to become a production-grade AI cloud platform as customers move AI workloads into deployment.

In 2027, the WSTS projects global semiconductor sales to reach $1.91 trillion, with memory revenue surpassing the $1 trillion mark and growing 32% YoY.

$MU $NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Global semiconductor sales could rise 90% YoY to $1.51 trillion, per the WSTS, a substantial revision from its prior forecast for $975 billion. 

Memory is expected to be the primary driver, up 250% to $803.9 billion, or more than 53% of the market. 

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO $MU

For comparison, the EPA estimates Americans use 3.3 trillion gallons of water each year to water lawns, meaning Amazon’s data center usage is less than 0.1% of that. 

$AMZN $NVDA $GOOG

OpenAI reportedly spent $34 billion last year, with $19 billion of that going towards R&D and roughly $6 billion going towards marketing, per the FT. 

$MSFT $AMZN $GOOG

📺 BEARS STAY IN CONTROL UNTIL THE GAPS GET FILLED + $MU EARNINGS TONIGHT WILL MAKE OR BREAK SEMIS

While many traders are asking whether leadership stocks breaking below their 21-day signals a larger trend change, the more important question is whether recent downside gaps remain open.

One of the strongest clues during the rally was a major upside gap that never filled. That gap helped confirm buyers were in control and supported the powerful advance that followed. 

Now the opposite may be developing. The recent break below key moving averages created downside gaps that have not yet been reclaimed. Until those gaps are filled, sellers retain the short-term advantage.

*

For $SPY, traders should focus on whether the market can reclaim the gap zone near $739 or eventually break below support around $732. 

If the gap remains open and support fails, it would strengthen the bearish case and suggest further downside pressure. 

If buyers can reclaim the gap, the recent weakness may prove to be just another pause within a larger uptrend.

*

The semiconductor sector remains the most important area to watch today. $SOXX $SOXL

It suffered a large downside gap that resembles an island-top type setup, trapping late buyers near the highs. 

The sector is attempting to bounce, but the key question is whether that bounce can hold. A weak rally that fades below the gap would suggest sellers remain in control and increase the odds of a deeper correction.

*

We have $MU earnings tonight.

Micron has become one of the most important AI infrastructure stocks due to surging demand for memory and higher pricing across the industry. 

The stock has already corrected roughly 20% from its highs, suggesting expectations have cooled somewhat, but investors are still looking for strong numbers and guidance.

If $MU delivers a powerful report and outlook, it could help semiconductors reclaim critical technical levels and invalidate some of the recent bearish signals. 

If the report disappoints or guidance fails to justify elevated expectations, the semiconductor correction could deepen and drag the broader AI trade lower.

And perhaps the entire market as well.

*

Until then, the charts remain clear: bears stay in control until the gaps get filled. Watch $MU tonight.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

U.S. equities are at record highs, rate cuts are being pushed further out, and AI-driven gains are concentrated in a narrow set of companies.

For investors fully exposed to U.S. equities, the question is no longer just about upside.

It's about what happens if sentiment shifts.

Join KraneShares CIO @ahern_brendan, @Hedgeye CEO @KeithMcCullough, and @HedgeyeAM CIO John McNamara III for a discussion on how to stay invested in U.S. equities while seeking to manage downside risk through today's elevated market environment.

The conversation will cover how $KSPY's dynamic, options-based approach seeks to offer a more responsive hedge than traditional buffer strategies, and how it has performed through recent volatility.

Register: rebrand.ly/b464a6
